stem: Acute pulmonary embolism with acute cor pulmonale:
rationale: I26.09 codes other pulmonary embolism with acute cor pulmonale. I26.01 is saddle embolus with acute cor pulmonale. I26.99 is without cor pulmonale.

stem: The principal procedure in ICD-10-PCS is defined as:
options: The procedure performed for definitive treatment most related to the principal diagnosis
rationale: The principal procedure is the one performed for definitive treatment of the principal diagnosis, or when none relates, the most resource-intensive.

stem: A patient with chronic pain syndrome. Correct code?
rationale: G89.4 codes chronic pain syndrome. G89.29 is other chronic pain. R52 is pain unspecified. G89.0 is central pain syndrome.

stem: RBRVS payment system is based on:
options: Relative value units for physician work, practice expense, and malpractice cost
rationale: RBRVS assigns relative value units (RVUs) for physician work, practice expense, and malpractice cost, multiplied by a conversion factor.

stem: An NEC (not elsewhere classifiable) code in ICD-10-CM means:
options: There is no specific code for the condition documented
A more specific code must be assigned
rationale: NEC indicates that ICD-10-CM lacks a specific code for the documented condition. The available code is the most specific option.

stem: Nosocomial Clostridioides difficile colitis developing during hospitalization. POA indicator?
rationale: A nosocomial C. diff infection that develops during hospitalization was not present on admission, so POA indicator is 'N'.

stem: Ambulatory payment classifications (APCs) may assign multiple APCs to:
options: A single outpatient encounter when multiple significant procedures are performed
rationale: Under OPPS, multiple APCs can be assigned to a single encounter when multiple significant separately payable procedures are performed.

stem: Informed consent in health records must document:
options: Nature of procedure, risks, benefits, alternatives, and patient agreement
rationale: Informed consent requires documentation of procedure nature, risks, benefits, alternatives, opportunity to ask questions, and patient agreement.

stem: The ICD-10-CM convention 'code first' means:
options: The underlying condition code must be sequenced before the manifestation code
Use the first code listed in the Index
rationale: 'Code first' instructs the coder to sequence the underlying condition before the manifestation code. This follows etiology/manifestation coding guidelines.

stem: Acute osteomyelitis of right tibia due to Staphylococcus aureus:
rationale: M86.161 codes acute osteomyelitis of right tibia. B95.61 identifies Staphylococcus aureus as the causative organism.

stem: Which HIPAA transaction standard is used for claim payment and remittance advice?
rationale: 835 is for electronic remittance advice (payment). 837 is claims submission. 270/271 is eligibility. 278 is referral authorization.

stem: A query for possible acute respiratory failure should include:
options: Clinical indicators such as ABG values, oxygen requirements, and ventilator settings
rationale: CDI queries should present objective clinical indicators (ABGs, O2 requirements, ventilator use) without leading to a specific diagnosis.

stem: ICD-10-CM 'use additional code' notation means:
options: An additional code should be assigned to provide more information about the condition
rationale: 'Use additional code' instructs coding of additional details such as organism, external cause, or manifestation for complete coding.

stem: A ROC curve in predictive analytics measures:
options: The tradeoff between sensitivity and specificity of a diagnostic test or model
rationale: ROC (Receiver Operating Characteristic) curves plot sensitivity vs. 1-specificity to evaluate the discriminative ability of a predictive model.

stem: Open biopsy of liver. PCS root operation and approach?
rationale: Biopsy is classified as Excision (B) since it removes a portion of tissue. Open approach is character value 0.

stem: The National Drug Code (NDC) identifies:
options: Specific drug products including labeler, product, and package size
rationale: NDC is a unique identifier for drugs with segments for labeler (manufacturer), product (strength/form), and package size.

stem: Type 1 DM with diabetic nephropathy:
rationale: E10.21 codes type 1 DM with diabetic nephropathy. E10.22 is with diabetic CKD. E11.21 is type 2 DM with nephropathy.

stem: Clinical documentation integrity requires documentation to be:
options: Accurate, complete, timely, specific, and legible
rationale: Clinical documentation must be accurate, complete, timely, specific, legible, and consistent to support proper coding and patient care.

stem: A frequency distribution table displays:
options: How often each value or category occurs in a dataset
rationale: Frequency distributions organize data by showing how often each value or category appears, enabling pattern identification.

stem: Acute tubular necrosis:
rationale: N17.0 codes acute kidney failure with tubular necrosis. N17.1 is with acute cortical necrosis. N17.2 is with medullary necrosis.

stem: An operative report must be dictated or written:
options: Immediately after surgery before the patient leaves the operating suite
rationale: TJC requires the operative report to be dictated or written immediately after surgery. A brief operative progress note is acceptable if the full report is dictated later.

stem: Status asthmaticus is classified as:
options: A life-threatening asthma exacerbation that does not respond to standard therapy
rationale: Status asthmaticus represents severe, life-threatening bronchospasm unresponsive to standard bronchodilator therapy, requiring emergency intervention.

stem: The correct code for encounter for immunization:
rationale: Z23 is the code for encounter for immunization. Z20 codes are for contact/exposure. Z11 is screening for infections. Z13 is screening for other diseases.

stem: In regression analysis, the dependent variable is:
options: The outcome variable being predicted or explained
The variable manipulated by the researcher
rationale: The dependent variable (outcome) is what is being predicted. Independent variables are the predictors used to explain variation.

stem: Modifier -TC indicates:
options: Technical component of a diagnostic test
rationale: Modifier -TC identifies the technical component (equipment, technician, facility) of a diagnostic test. -26 is the professional component.

stem: CMS-1500 form is used for:
rationale: CMS-1500 is the standard claim form for professional and physician services. UB-04 is for institutional claims.

stem: Hypertensive urgency:
rationale: I16.0 codes hypertensive urgency. I16.1 is hypertensive emergency. I10 is essential hypertension. I11.0 is hypertensive heart disease with HF.

stem: A physician orders should include:
options: Date, time, specific instructions, and physician signature/authentication
rationale: Complete physician orders require date, time, specific instructions, dosage/frequency (for medications), and physician authentication.

stem: The root operation 'Supplement' in PCS means:
options: Putting in a biological or synthetic material to reinforce or augment a body part
Moving a body part to a new location
rationale: Supplement (U) puts in biological or synthetic material to physically reinforce or augment a body part, such as hernia mesh.

stem: A p-value less than 0.05 in a statistical test indicates:
options: Statistical significance; the result is unlikely due to chance alone
rationale: A p-value <0.05 indicates statistical significance, meaning there is less than a 5% probability the result occurred by chance alone.

stem: Code Z87.11 represents:
options: Personal history of peptic ulcer disease
rationale: Z87.11 codes personal history of peptic ulcer disease. Personal history codes indicate a past condition no longer present.

stem: Emancipated minor health records are governed by:
options: The same privacy rules as adult records
rationale: Emancipated minors have the same rights as adults regarding their health information, including privacy and consent to treatment.

stem: A clinical trial phase III tests:
options: Efficacy and safety in a large patient population compared to standard treatment
rationale: Phase III trials test efficacy and safety in large populations, often randomized controlled trials comparing to standard treatment or placebo.

stem: Encounter for antineoplastic radiation therapy, principal diagnosis:
rationale: Z51.0 is the principal diagnosis when the encounter is solely for radiation therapy. The malignancy code is assigned as additional.

stem: Healthcare fraud is differentiated from abuse by:
options: Intent to deceive for financial gain
rationale: Fraud requires intentional deception or misrepresentation for unauthorized benefit. Abuse is inconsistent with accepted practices but lacks intent.

stem: Acute exacerbation of chronic systolic heart failure:
rationale: I50.23 codes acute on chronic systolic (left ventricular) heart failure, capturing both the acute and chronic nature.

stem: A DRG grouper software:
options: Assigns the appropriate MS-DRG based on coded diagnoses and procedures
rationale: DRG grouper software uses coded diagnoses, procedures, age, sex, and discharge status to assign the appropriate MS-DRG for reimbursement.

stem: Non-healing surgical wound, subsequent encounter:
rationale: T81.89XD codes other complications of procedures, subsequent encounter. The 7th character D indicates subsequent encounter for routine healing.

stem: Protected health information includes:
options: Any individually identifiable health information transmitted or maintained in any form
rationale: PHI includes any health information in any form (electronic, paper, oral) that identifies an individual and relates to health condition, care, or payment.

stem: In a relational database, a foreign key:
options: Links a record in one table to a record in another table
rationale: A foreign key creates a relationship between tables by referencing the primary key of another table, enabling data integrity and relational queries.

stem: Atrial flutter:
rationale: I48.92 codes unspecified atrial flutter. I48.91 is unspecified atrial fibrillation. I48.0 is paroxysmal AF. I48.20 is chronic AF.

stem: The term 'work queue' in coding operations refers to:
options: A list of accounts requiring coding action organized by priority
rationale: Work queues organize coding assignments by priority, discharge date, or payer, allowing efficient workflow management.

stem: Malignant ascites:
rationale: R18.0 codes malignant ascites. R18.8 is other ascites. C78.6 is secondary malignant neoplasm of retroperitoneum and peritoneum.

stem: Zero-based budgeting in HIM requires:
options: Justifying all expenses from zero each budget cycle rather than using previous year as base
Using last year's budget as the starting point
rationale: Zero-based budgeting requires all costs to be justified for each new period, regardless of previous budgets, promoting efficiency.

stem: Encounter for fitting and adjustment of insulin pump:
rationale: Z46.81 codes encounter for fitting and adjustment of insulin pump. Z46 codes are for fitting and adjustment of devices.

stem: A root cause analysis (RCA) in healthcare quality:
options: Identifies the underlying causes of adverse events to prevent recurrence
rationale: RCA systematically investigates underlying causes of adverse events, near-misses, or sentinel events to implement corrective actions.

stem: Newborn affected by maternal gestational diabetes:
rationale: P70.0 codes syndrome of infant of mother with gestational diabetes. P70.1 is infant of diabetic mother (pre-existing). P codes are perinatal.

stem: Token-based authentication in health IT:
options: Uses digital tokens to verify user identity without transmitting passwords
rationale: Token-based authentication provides secure access by generating digital tokens after initial verification, reducing password exposure.

stem: Central line-associated bloodstream infection (CLABSI) is reported as:
options: A hospital-acquired condition that may affect reimbursement under CMS rules
rationale: CLABSI is a CMS hospital-acquired condition. When not POA, it may prevent higher DRG payment, incentivizing prevention.

stem: Acute pancreatitis with peripancreatic necrosis without infection:
rationale: K85.90 codes acute pancreatitis, unspecified, without necrosis or infection. Specific codes exist for different etiologies and complications.

stem: The concept of interoperability in healthcare means:
options: Different health IT systems can exchange, interpret, and use data seamlessly
Systems operate independently without data sharing
rationale: Interoperability enables different IT systems to exchange data, interpret meaning, and use information for coordinated patient care.

stem: Catheter-associated urinary tract infection, initial encounter:
rationale: T83.511A codes infection due to indwelling urethral catheter, initial encounter. N39.0 may also be assigned for the UTI.

stem: In project management, a critical path identifies:
options: The longest sequence of tasks determining the minimum project duration
rationale: The critical path is the longest sequence of dependent tasks, determining the minimum project duration. Delays on this path delay the project.

stem: Acute ST elevation myocardial infarction involving left main coronary artery:
rationale: I21.01 codes STEMI involving left main coronary artery. I21.02 is LAD. I21.09 is other anterior wall. I21.11 is right coronary artery.

stem: A compliance program must include:
options: Written policies, training, auditing, reporting mechanism, and corrective action
rationale: OIG recommends seven elements: written standards, compliance officer, training, reporting mechanism, auditing, enforcement, and corrective action.

stem: Sacral decubitus ulcer, stage 4:
rationale: L89.154 codes pressure ulcer of sacral region, stage 4. The 6th character specifies staging: 4 indicates full-thickness skin loss with exposed bone/tendon.

stem: The claims clearinghouse function is to:
options: Receive claims from providers, scrub and format them, and transmit to appropriate payers
rationale: Clearinghouses serve as intermediaries that receive, edit, reformat, and route electronic claims between providers and payers.

stem: Secondary malignant neoplasm of liver:
rationale: C78.7 codes secondary (metastatic) malignant neoplasm of liver. C22.0 is primary hepatocellular carcinoma. C22.9 is liver cancer unspecified.

stem: Physician attestation for documentation purposes means:
options: The physician confirms the accuracy and completeness of the documentation by signature
The physician delegates all documentation to nursing staff
The physician reviews but does not sign the record
The physician only reviews medication orders
rationale: Physician attestation requires signature or electronic authentication confirming the documentation is accurate, complete, and reflects the care provided.

stem: An observation stay is:
options: A short-term outpatient status used to determine if inpatient admission is necessary
rationale: Observation status is outpatient, used to evaluate patients to determine if inpatient admission is needed, typically less than 48 hours.

stem: Hypothyroidism, unspecified:
rationale: E03.9 codes unspecified hypothyroidism. E01.8 is iodine-deficiency related. E02 is subclinical iodine-deficiency. E06.3 is autoimmune thyroiditis.

stem: Point-of-care testing documentation should include:
options: Test name, result, date/time, operator identification, and quality control data
rationale: Point-of-care testing documentation requires test identification, results, date/time, operator ID, quality control data, and action taken.

stem: Coding productivity is typically measured by:
options: Number of charts coded per hour or per day with accuracy benchmarks
rationale: Coding productivity metrics include charts coded per hour/day with accuracy rate targets (typically 95%+ accuracy).

stem: Chronic kidney disease, stage 5:
rationale: N18.5 codes CKD stage 5. N18.4 is stage 4. N18.6 is end-stage renal disease. N18.9 is unspecified CKD.

stem: The case management process in healthcare includes:
options: Assessment, planning, implementation, coordination, monitoring, and evaluation
rationale: Case management encompasses comprehensive assessment, care planning, implementation, service coordination, monitoring, and outcome evaluation.

stem: Bilateral knee osteoarthritis, primary:
rationale: M17.0 codes bilateral primary osteoarthritis of knee. M17.11 is primary right knee. M17.12 is primary left knee.

stem: Risk adjustment models in healthcare are used to:
options: Account for differences in patient severity when comparing outcomes across providers
Adjust billing rates based on geography
rationale: Risk adjustment accounts for patient acuity differences, enabling fair comparison of outcomes, costs, and resource utilization across providers.

stem: Open excision of right breast lesion. PCS body part value:
rationale: The body part value specifies right breast for the excision. Laterality is captured directly in the body part character of the PCS code.

stem: Readmission within 30 days for the same condition may result in:
options: Reduced Medicare reimbursement under the Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program
rationale: CMS Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program penalizes hospitals with higher-than-expected readmission rates for specified conditions.

stem: Neonatal jaundice due to prematurity:
rationale: P59.0 codes neonatal jaundice associated with preterm delivery. P59.9 is unspecified. P58.9 is neonatal jaundice due to other specified excessive hemolysis.

stem: The Conditions of Participation (CoP) are:
options: Federal requirements that healthcare facilities must meet to participate in Medicare/Medicaid
rationale: CoPs are federal requirements established by CMS that hospitals and other providers must meet for Medicare/Medicaid participation.

stem: Sacroiliitis:
rationale: M46.1 codes sacroiliitis, not elsewhere classified. M45 is ankylosing spondylitis. M54.5 is low back pain.

stem: A Pareto chart is used to:
options: Identify the most significant factors contributing to a problem by showing frequency in descending order
rationale: Pareto charts display factors in descending frequency with a cumulative line, identifying the vital few causes of problems (80/20 rule).

stem: Alcohol use disorder, moderate, in early remission:
rationale: F10.20 codes alcohol dependence, uncomplicated. For remission status, F10.21 is in remission. Clinical documentation specifies severity.

stem: Under HIPAA, a patient can request an accounting of disclosures for the past:
rationale: HIPAA gives patients the right to receive an accounting of PHI disclosures made in the prior 6 years (or since the compliance date).

stem: ICD-10-CM code for BMI 35.0-35.9:
rationale: Z68.35 codes BMI 35.0-35.9 in adults. BMI codes are always additional codes, never principal or first-listed.

stem: A fishbone (Ishikawa) diagram is used in quality improvement to:
options: Identify potential root causes of a problem organized by categories
rationale: Fishbone diagrams organize potential causes into categories (people, process, equipment, etc.) to systematically identify root causes.

stem: Post-thyroidectomy hypocalcemia, initial encounter:
rationale: E89.2 codes postprocedural hypoparathyroidism leading to hypocalcemia. E89.0 is postprocedural hypothyroidism.

stem: An enterprise master patient index (EMPI) differs from a standard MPI by:
options: Linking patient records across multiple facilities within a healthcare system
Tracking records within a single facility only
rationale: EMPI links patient identities across multiple facilities and systems within an enterprise, enabling unified patient identification.

stem: Acute diverticulitis of large intestine with perforation and abscess:
rationale: K57.20 codes diverticulitis of large intestine with perforation and abscess. K57.00 is small intestine. The 5th character differentiates location.

stem: Time-limited access to PHI should be granted based on:
options: The principle of minimum necessary for the specific task or role
rationale: Time-limited access follows the minimum necessary principle, granting PHI access only for the duration needed for a specific authorized purpose.

stem: A benchmarking study in healthcare compares:
options: Performance metrics against best practices or peer organizations
rationale: Benchmarking compares organizational performance metrics against industry best practices, peer institutions, or established standards.

stem: Type 2 DM with diabetic retinopathy with macular edema, right eye:
rationale: E11.3211 codes type 2 DM with mild nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy with macular edema, right eye. Extended characters capture laterality.

stem: The PDCA (Plan-Do-Check-Act) cycle is used for:
options: Continuous quality improvement through iterative problem-solving
rationale: PDCA is a continuous improvement methodology: Plan (identify problem), Do (implement), Check (evaluate), Act (standardize or adjust).

stem: Periprosthetic fracture around right hip prosthesis, initial encounter:
rationale: M97.01XA codes periprosthetic fracture around internal prosthetic right hip joint, initial encounter.

stem: A sentinel event is defined as:
options: An unexpected occurrence involving death or serious physical or psychological injury
rationale: TJC defines sentinel events as unexpected occurrences involving death, permanent harm, or severe temporary harm requiring intervention.

stem: NSTEMI (non-ST elevation myocardial infarction):
rationale: I21.4 codes non-ST elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI). I21.3 is STEMI of unspecified site. I25.2 is old MI.

stem: Concurrent coding review is performed:
options: During the patient's hospital stay before discharge
rationale: Concurrent review occurs during hospitalization, allowing real-time documentation improvement and query resolution before discharge.

stem: Single live birth, delivered by cesarean:
rationale: Z37.0 is outcome code for single live birth (maternal record only). O82 indicates delivery by cesarean. Z38.01 is for newborn record.

stem: Health record documentation must be completed within:
options: 30 days of discharge per TJC and CMS requirements
rationale: TJC and CMS require medical records to be completed within 30 days following patient discharge, including all signatures and dictations.

stem: In PCS, the root operation 'Change' means:
options: Taking out or off a device and putting back an identical or similar device without cutting
rationale: Change (2) takes out or off a device and puts back an identical or similar device without making an incision or puncture.

stem: Myelodysplastic syndrome, unspecified:
rationale: D46.9 codes unspecified myelodysplastic syndrome. D46.0 is refractory anemia. C94.6 is myelodysplastic disease.

stem: Value-based purchasing (VBP) ties hospital reimbursement to:
options: Quality performance measures and patient outcomes
rationale: VBP links reimbursement to quality metrics including clinical outcomes, patient experience, safety, and efficiency measures.

stem: Bilateral pneumonia, organism unspecified:
rationale: J18.1 codes lobar pneumonia, unspecified organism. J18.9 is pneumonia unspecified. Bilateral pneumonia may use specific organism codes if identified.

stem: A telehealth modifier on a CPT code indicates:
options: The service was provided via real-time audio/video technology
The service was provided in an emergency room
The service was for a minor
rationale: Telehealth modifiers (modifier -95 or POS 02) indicate services delivered via real-time interactive telecommunication technology.

stem: Normocytic anemia:
rationale: D64.9 codes unspecified anemia (often normocytic). D50.9 is iron deficiency. D51.9 is vitamin B12 deficiency. D53.9 is nutritional anemia.

stem: Under the Stark Law, designated health services include:
options: Clinical laboratory, radiology, DME, physical therapy, and inpatient/outpatient hospital services
rationale: Stark Law DHS include clinical lab, imaging, DME, physical therapy, occupational therapy, radiation therapy, and hospital services.

stem: Postoperative ileus:
rationale: K91.89 codes other postprocedural complications of digestive system, which may include postoperative ileus. K56.0 is paralytic ileus.

stem: A mortality index in healthcare statistics measures:
options: The proportion of deaths among a specific patient population
rationale: Mortality index compares actual deaths to expected deaths based on case severity, used for quality benchmarking.

stem: Encounter for palliative care:
rationale: Z51.5 codes encounter for palliative care. This is an additional code, not principal. The underlying condition is coded first.
